category_title: Oppositional and boisterous students with disruptive behaviors
minimal_context: The class is sitting on the carpet after lunch listening to a read-aloud of 'Ereth's Birthday'.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Alright everyone, let's continue reading 'Ereth's Birthday' on the carpet. Ereth just said his usual grumpy phrase. What do we think about what just happened?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Repeats the phrase from the book, guffaws, and looks around at the other students) Um, unnecessary?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's keep reading to see what happens to him next.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Fidgets, faces away from the teacher, and makes a funny face to get another student's attention) He didn’t get killed. ... You mean the cage trap.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please remember to raise your hand if you want to share your thoughts.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Laughs loudly and interrupts again) No! ... The weak don’t always have to help the strong because if they’re a criminal or something, that might make it worse.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Alright everyone, let's continue reading 'Ereth's Birthday' on the carpet. Ereth just said his usual grumpy phrase. What do we think about what just happened?

STUDENT: (Repeats the phrase from the book, guffaws, and looks around at the other students) Um, unnecessary?

TUTOR: Let's keep reading to see what happens to him next.

STUDENT: (Fidgets, faces away from the teacher, and makes a funny face to get another student's attention) He didn’t get killed. ... You mean the cage trap.

TUTOR: Please remember to raise your hand if you want to share your thoughts.

STUDENT: (Laughs loudly and interrupts again) No! ... The weak don’t always have to help the strong because if they’re a criminal or something, that might make it worse.

task.content: Listening to and discussing the book 'Ereth’s Birthday' and Greek mythology

context.environment: Whole-class read aloud on the carpet
context.materials: Book ('Ereth’s Birthday'), book about Greek mythology
context.social_setting: Whole group sitting together on the carpet

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the teacher pronounces phrases from the book or when other students raise their hands to speak
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Repeats phrases, guffaws, looks around hoping to enlist others in silliness. Interrupts 17 times during a single read aloud to say something silly, laugh loudly, or make a smart-aleck comment.
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Hoping to enlist others in the silliness
related_quotes: He didn’t get killed. ... You mean the cage trap.
No! ... The weak don’t always have to help the strong because if they’re a criminal or something, that might make it worse.

behavior_patterns.condition: During read-aloud time on the carpet
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Fidgeting, facing away from the teacher, and trying to get the attention of other students through funny faces, gestures, and noises.
